activation_results.reason: Input matches the description's canonical use case verbatim.
Produce is a direct synonym for Generate and the rest of the phrase matches the description.
User invokes the skill by its exact kebab-case name.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to Google Ads copy generation.
Asking about skills in general is meta and not a request to generate ad copy.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'cosy', 'fibre', 'Aussie', 'Australian'; no US spellings detected anywhere in the artefact.

criterion_results.text: Output is machine-readable where the description implies (valid JSON/SQL/YAML)
criterion_results.evidence: Description implies structured ad copy with character/pin rules; artefact uses well-formed markdown tables with explicit char counts and pin slots — the canonical machine-parseable format for Google Ads asset specs.
Both headings present verbatim at lines 67 (## Headlines (15)) and 95 (## Descriptions (4)) with complete tables underneath.

judge_result.notes: Artefact is a well-structured Google Ads copy brief in Australian English with all canonical sections: 15 headlines with pin assignments and rationale, 4 descriptions, two path fields with character counts, 6 sitelinks, and 5 callouts, each with character-count validation. Character/pin rules implied by the description are honoured throughout (headlines under 30, descriptions under 90, callouts under 25, paths under 15). All three judge criteria are met.
